codeReview.summary: The feature implements the VllmBackend trait surface and factory wiring, but two lifecycle correctness issues remain: adopt() does not validate the specific persisted container_id, and stop() suppresses compose-down failures while reporting success.

issues.description: adopt() only checks whether any container for the compose project is running (is_container_running via `docker compose ps -q`) and then stores the caller-provided container_id at line 429. It never verifies that the provided/persisted container_id itself is running, so stale or mismatched IDs can be adopted and persisted as if valid.

stop() discards errors from `docker compose down` (`let _ = ...`) and always clears internal state/returns Ok. If compose down fails, the daemon can report Stopped while the container is still running, breaking lifecycle correctness.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit 57a814c diff. VllmBackend lifecycle is mostly implemented, but adopt() and stop() contain blocking correctness gaps that can cause stale/misreported runtime state, so this review is marked fail.
